raw_input:
[3.0/5] just like mousse or shaving cream, but is a soap for body The bottle and texture is very much like a hair mousse or shaving cream, but is a wash for the body. Instead of a pump or squeeze bottle, this comes out though as pressure aerosol - also just like a mousse or shaving cream. The ingredients necessary to make this happen are Butane and Propane. They are aerosol propellents. If you have ever used mousse, hair spray, various perfumes/body sprays, air fresheners, aerosol deodorants, or shaving cream,etc.. then you are already familiar with these ingredients. They are generally recognized as safe, and are used in small, diluted quantities in skin care. Fun fact, "These ingredients are used in cosmetics and personal care products as a replacements for chlorofluorocarbons, or CFC propellants, some of which have been shown to have negative effects on the environment". I'm not a big fan of slathering my body with butane, propane, or isobutane, but I understand their use in this type of medium. So it's up to you to decide.

This company does have non aerosol body wash available too..

The resulting foamy wash comes out thick, it is creamy. Because it reminds me so much of a shaving cream, that it what I have been using it for. Then whatever is left over I use on rest of body. Is pleasant smelling enough to me but it isn't quite a pure aromatherapy experience for me. Clearly it uses a variety of essential oils- rosemary, orange, eucalyptus, peppermint, limonene. However, for whatever reason, it contains "parfum (fragrance)" as an added ingredient. As well as Alpha-Isomethyl Ionone, another synthetic fragrance ingredient. Is there not enough naturally occurring scent from the use of essential oils? I think the added synthetic fragrance is redundant and takes away from the olfactory experience.

As an actual wash, it is fine.
3 stars-- this is just a middle of the road rating. I am not in love. The experience is underwhelming. I am much too quickly going through the container. Aromatherapy experience is a little off as I pick up on something other than essential oils. Otherwise the cleansing action is fine. But.. I likely won't repurchase.

I think if I really want a foam wash, I will just use another body wash or make my own wash and pour into a foaming soap dispenser.

CRUELTY FREE | NO PARAFFIN, SILICONE OR MINERAL OILS | PLANT-BASED | PARABEN FREE | VEGAN

Aqua (Water), Sodium Laureth Sulfate, Butane, Glycerin, PEG-40 Hydrogenated Castor Oil, Isopropyl Palmitate, Prunus Amygdalus Dulcis (Sweet Almond) Oil, Mentha Aquatica Extract, Rosmarinus Officinalis (Rosemary) Oil, Citrus Aurantium Dulcis (Orange) Peel Oil, Eucalyptus Globulus Leaf Oil, Mentha Piperita (Peppermint) Oil, Linalool, Limonene, Benzyl Alcohol, Geraniol, Alpha-Isomethyl Ionone, Sodium Levulinate, Sodium Anisate, Parfum (Fragrance), Hydroxyethylcellulose, Sorbitol, Propylene Glycol, Glucose, Propane, Isobutane, Citric Acid, Lactic Acid, Sodium Hydroxide [5.0/5] I like the scent Just tried this out on my hands so that I could see how it works and smells. It smells good. The smell does dissipate after time. I am not so sure how I feel about a foam for my body, but I will adjust. When I wash, I prefer to use a pouf and this will work with gel or foam, so I won't even notice. I think I am going to be liking this and looking for more [4.0/5] Refreshing Scent, Easy to Use The only thing I didn't like about this shower "foam" is how quickly it will be used up. It comes in a relatively small caninster and it comes out like shaving foam, only it's for the body and not for the face. It feels great and helps clear my stuffed sinuses. The scent is "mint and rosemary" but I feel like it's mostly mint. It's a step up above your ordinary shower gel but it won't likely last as long, as it's hard to tell how much of the bottle you are actually using since it's not liquid. Except for that caveat, I liked this. [4.0/5] Nice! I have been a fan of this brand since I discovered it as a teen. The shower wash is nice. It smells lovely, has a great texture, and it leaves my skin soft and not dry. I really, really like this! I just wish it came in a larger package. [3.0/5] Feels and washes lovely - smells like MEN'S COLOGNE, though :( This wash is luxurious. Just a couple of pumps was all I needed for my shower. The directions say to use your hand and rub in but I used a pouf and it worked well. The foam really is luxurious and goes a long way.

My problem is the very strong scent. In no way does it smell like mint or rosemary (two scents that I LOVE!). To me, it smells like a men's cologne. In fact it smells just like this hand wash that a male friend keeps in his bathroom. I think guys would probably like this very much - me, not really. The scent is also VERY strong while using - like VERY. The good news is that it leaves just the lightest scent on your cleansed skin - thank goodness. Just very light.

It left my skin clean and soft. Too bad I really do not like the scent as I will likely not be using it again. : ( Sad too as I truly appreciate how it is clearly marked Vegan as that is important to me. If you found my review helpful, why not click the "Helpful" button. Thanks! : ) [5.0/5] Soothing I absolutely love it just a little foam goes a very long way! Refreshing [5.0/5] Smells Great, Good Foaming I have been enjoying this body foam. The smell isn't too overwhelming, and honestly I could use a bit more smell. It foams up really well, and glides on the skin. You can use not a lot and still cover your entire body. It washes off nicely, and you don't really smell it afterward. I have been using this for about a week and the container hasn't rusted or anything either, which is something that tends to happen with products in a metal container. I wish there was a way to know how much was left, so I know when to get a new one, but overall I am happy with this body wash. [3.0/5] Great smell and great lather, not so sure about the ingredients. This body wash not only smells great but provides a thick silky lather. I generally don't enjoy using foaming body washes because they don't foam up enough to get a good lather going so I have to use more of it than I want to; that is not a problem when using this! Two squirts of foam and I can really work up a respectable lather all while basking in the wonderful scent of rosemary and water mint!

With that being said, I don't think I'll be purchasing this after using my current bottle just because the ingredients are VERY questionable. I'm very conscientious about what I put on and into my body and I try to stay as natural as possible. There are some very questionable ingredients in this body wash you should watch out for including butane, isopropyl palmitate, propane, sodium hydroxide, and benzyl alcohol, all of which I'd rather not have going on my skin and into my body. I understand that isopropyl palmitate is what causes that rich lather I spoke so highly about earlier but be aware it can problematic for some people. I'm also not sure what the purpose of butane and propane is in this product...maybe for aerosolization purposes? If that's the case I'd much rather see a pump used on this product to create and propel the foam like some other foaming washes. [3.0/5] Good and soapy I like the foamy milky soap but not too crazy about the scent… doesn’t smell bad just not my favorite. I’ll try other scents. [5.0/5] Fun, not strong Unlike other Kneipp products, this one didn't have a strong scent. I do like that it doesn't leave a smell on my skin so that I can wear my own perfume after.
Foam comes out a lot and has nice feel. [5.0/5] A wonderful bathing treat -- use it sparingly and it goes a long way! Sad to learn that Kneipp is discontinuing this wonderful product. They have had 4 different shower foam soaps in their lineup, my favorite being the Men's version (now discontinued). I think the main problem for most customers is that they use too much and then it runs out quickly. You really don't need much!

My technique is to use other inexpensive soaps first and then hit the "prime spots" with Kneipp (pits, privates, rear), as well as neck and face. The aroma with this one is really relaxing! [5.0/5] Yo! Stress relief isn’t the word. The scent alone calms my body. [[VIDEOID:522558cd2a0f5dd82f1651d9632e07fe]] This is an awesome foam body wash. I loved every minute of bathing with this. I do not use it on my private area only my surrounding parts. I rather use sensitive skin soap for those delicate areas. This foam is actually gentle on my skin and doesn’t dry me out. It smells wonderful and have my body relaxed after a steamy hot shower with this. Whoever created this scent knew what they were doing and should keep doing it for hard working mothers such as myself that needs products like this one to relax❣️ [5.0/5] Luxurious The aroma is very relaxing, and the foam leaves my skin feeling soft and refrehed. [4.0/5] Smells GREAT, quite pricey tho Lovely scent, not too overpowering like some scented bath products. Quite expensive in my opinion. [5.0/5] The scent is so good, really practical change from gel to foam. I really enjoyed this soap. The scent is so good, its so practical change from gel to foam. I guess I used it too much for the first time, I only need 1 pump and spread it with loofah and that's enough for the whole body. [3.0/5] Super foamy! [[VIDEOID:69e74be6ba26ae9d29daee25655928f7]] It's a nice mousy body wash. The sent is great and super relaxing in the shower. It's hydrating too. It's just that it's not something that will last you months. It's better than my lever body wash but that lever one lasts me forever. Because of the formula, it would be perfect to shave your legs. Overall, it's ok. [5.0/5] Lucious Foam Luscious thick foamy wash. I have another foam wash and this one seems way more moisturizing. I like the way it feels when washing, it don't feel like I have to use so much of it as I do my other wash. The smell is fantastic to me. Very light and not over-powering. [5.0/5] Smells SO good! I love this stuff! It smells really refreshing. I'm into essential oils and it smells just like that...strong but invigorating. It comes out just like shaving cream foam. Scent lasts after you leave the bath/shower. 10/10 recommend [5.0/5] The way it makes me feel…… I have been using this Shower Foam for a few years and I totally love it.

response:
This foaming body wash from Kneipp gets mostly positive reviews, with customers frequently praising its refreshing scent and luxurious foam. The rosemary, mint, eucalyptus, and orange essential oil blend is a highlight for many, with several reviewers noting it feels like an aromatherapy experience and helps clear sinuses. Most agree that a little goes a long way, which helps offset the higher price point some find hard to justify.

The foam texture is generally well-liked, often compared to shaving cream, and many say it leaves skin feeling soft and clean. Several reviewers note it works well with a loofah or pouf. A few customers mention the container rusting over time as a minor issue.

The main criticisms center on the aerosol-style delivery system, which contains butane, propane, and isobutane as propellants. Some buyers who prefer natural products take issue with these ingredients, as well as the inclusion of synthetic fragrance alongside the natural essential oils. A handful of reviewers also find the scent too strong or cologne-like rather than purely herbal.

Some customers are disappointed that the product runs out quickly, and a few note that Kneipp appears to be discontinuing some varieties in the line. Price is occasionally mentioned as a drawback.

Overall, most buyers enjoy the product and would recommend it, particularly for those who enjoy a strong, invigorating scent and a rich lather in the shower.
